body:
Builds off @vchuravy's work in #261.

Preliminary design of user API for concrete models. Probably most of the time the user will have to specify boundary conditions prior to building the model. We'll have to come up with good syntax for that. What we have is ok, but can be improved.

The checkpointing tests currently fail, I think because they attempt to change the types of the some of the model fields.
title: Be more concrete

body: Requires https://github.com/JuliaGPU/CUDAdrv.jl/pull/144 and https://github.com/JuliaGPU/CUDAnative.jl/pull/416 (i.e. master of both packages).
title: Dynamic launch config.

body: `launch_config` could probably be generalized with an `arch` argument to work on both GPUs and CPUs, but I'll leave that to you guys.
title: Use a dynamic launch config for more kernels

body:
Reverts climate-machine/Oceananigans.jl#263 because boundary conditions API broke. Seems that the tests did not catch this maybe?

title: Revert "Be more concrete"

body:
cc @sandreza

body: Cube blocks should facilitate shared memory reuse.
title: Generalize indices in the z dimension
